text:
In the present review, our objective was to scrutinize the validity of using Folin-Ciocalteu (F-C) assay as the common benchmark for assessing the amount of phenolics in natural origin plants and plant derived food materials. This test was derived from Folin-Denis protein test reagent of 19th century. It consists of oxidation-reduction between the phenolic moieties in which oxidation of phenolic structure takes place and along with it reduction of a mixture of molybdenum and tungsten heteropoly acid takes place yielding a blue color product.

Two analytical strategies were investigated, flow through SPE with subsequent determination by a dual-reagent or a single-reagent colorimetric method. Our investigation showed that the use of a single-reagent method gave more precise results, higher sensitivity, and that the analysis time was significantly shorter and less affected by interference from proteins and amino acids. Thus, a precision of 3.1% average was obtained with the single-reagent method in comparison to a precision of 9.7% with the dual-reagent method.

The paper also deals with other current problems with the F-C assay method, i.e. the problem of chemical interference and bound phenolic compounds. We have made some important improvements in order to increase the selectivity of the assay method. The interferences from non-phenolic reducing agents such as vitamin C and sugars were eliminated by using solid phase extraction (SPE) with Oasis HLB or Polyclar AT resins. A new hydrolysis procedure was worked out for the determination of the "total" content of phenolic compounds which are generally believed to be underestimated because they are present as ethers or esters bound to the rest of the molecule. Some aspects of modern developments of the F-C assay method, e.g. use of semi-automated microplate reader, flow injection analysis and determination of urinary flavonoids as biomarkers for fruit and vegetables consumption, are also discussed. In this way the F-C assay, an excellent and competitive method regarding both price and accuracy, is still of great importance for the antioxidant research.
